title: Canonical Manuscript
description: A canonical manuscript witness record (FRBR Manifestation+Item collapsed). A specific physical codex/scroll/fragment, identifiable by shelf-mark in a specific library. Distinct from Work (the abstract intellectual entity). Forward-declared in v0.1.0; activation in P0.3 with the Ottoman HTR pilot (kadı sicilleri 33,149 records, 79 defters) and Süleymaniye / TSMK / British Library digital catalog crosswalks.

labels.description: Manuscript label — usually the conventional shelf-mark format, e.g., 'Süleymaniye, Şehid Ali Paşa 1825'. Title of contained work goes in witnesses_work, not in labels.

witnesses_work.description: Work(s) witnessed by this manuscript. Typically one but may be multiple for majmū'a (composite) manuscripts. Bidirectional with work.extant_manuscripts.

current_library.description: PID of the place (specific library) where the manuscript is currently held. The library itself is a iac:place- of subtype Settlement (or specific iac:institution- in P1).

shelf_mark.description: Library-internal shelf mark, e.g., 'Şehid Ali Paşa 1825', 'BNF Arabe 5847', 'BL Add. 23,392'. Verbatim form including space/punctuation as the holding library uses.

folio_count.description: Number of folios. Single-folio fragments use 1.

dating_temporal.description: When the manuscript was copied. Often known to a single year (colophon) or a quarter-century range (paleographic dating).

script.description: Script style. Controlled vocabulary based on standard Arabic/Persian/Ottoman paleography. Multi-script manuscripts use the dominant script in this field and additional scripts in note.

scribe.description: PID of scribe (when known from colophon).

patron.description: PID of patron who commissioned the copy.

previous_owners.description: Known prior owners (from ownership stamps, marginalia, or vakıf certificates).

language.description: Languages present in the manuscript. Multi-valued for multilingual manuscripts (e.g., ['ar', 'fa', 'ota'] for an Ottoman-era trilingual codex).

physical_description.description: Free-text physical description: support material (paper / parchment), binding, illumination notes, condition. Codicological detail.

digitization_url.description: URL to a digital surrogate (manifest, IIIF, viewer). Preferred form: IIIF presentation manifest URI.

iiif_manifest.description: IIIF Presentation API manifest URI, when available. Distinct from digitization_url to allow tooling to dispatch directly.

$comment: Phase 0.3 hard rules: (a) shelf_mark + current_library together must be unique across the catalog; (b) witnesses_work required; (c) at least one dating_temporal anchor required.
